name: Pickled Beets
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the beets and leave at least one inch of stems intact.
2. Cook the beets in water with a little bit of salt until tender.
3. Drain the beets and cover them in cold water to cool, then peel off the skins.
4. Slice or cube the beets as desired.
5. In a saucepan, mix all the other ingredients (sugar, water, cider vinegar, lemon juice, cinnamon, cloves, and allspice) and bring to a boil.
6. Add the sliced or cubed beets to the saucepan and bring back to a boil.
7. Remove from heat and let cool before transferring the beets and syrup to bottles.
8. Store in a refrigerator; these pickled beets can be eaten the same day without needing additional curing time.
Note: This recipe is inspired by "The New Brunswick Women's Cook Book".
